question_text: A student heats 50 grams of ice at 0°C. After adding 1000 joules of heat energy, some of the ice melts. If melting ice requires 334 joules per gram, how many grams of ice melted? (Give your answer to 1 decimal place.)
explanation: Use the formula: mass melted = heat energy / energy per gram = 1000 / 334 = 2.99 grams. This tests the ability to apply the specific latent heat concept in a calculation, which is at the upper end of KS3 but within Year 8 stretch.
